meaning: n.沉闷的金属声v.发出沉闷声
examples: Something fell to the floor with a clunk.
I heard a clunk on the roof. Must be the reindeer.
The loud clunk vibrated the boat temporarily, before water started to trickle slowly over the stern.
Other public health messages are short and snappy, like 'clunk-click every trip' or 'catch it bin it kill it'.
It can be heard every quarter of an hour as a clunk.
